acceleration_and_curvature.curvature_interpretation: R is exactly the original scalar evaluated at phi'=phi-beta(t); beta_dot and beta_double_dot cancel
acceleration_and_curvature.scope: coordinate/frame acceleration in the conditional 1+1 depth slice, not a force or equivalence principle

adjudication.accelerating_observers: ARBITRARY_SMOOTH_BETA_FRAME_PATHS_SUPPORTED KINEMATICALLY; PHYSICAL OBSERVER INTERPRETATION REQUIRES THE TIMELIKE BOUND; BETA_DOUBLE_DOT ENTERS LEVI_CIVITA_CONNECTION

depth_connection.absorbed_pullback_shift: in fixed unprimed coordinates, dphi'=dphi+a_shift with a_shift=-d beta
depth_connection.local_recenter: phi->phi-beta(x); a->a+d beta keeps Dphi invariant
depth_connection.not_claimed: independent connection field, force, nonzero holonomy, or dynamics
depth_connection.sign_convention: a_prime and a_shift are different representations and must not be identified

dynamic_composition.global_counterexample: beta(t)=t^2 on R maps time only onto (-sqrt(pi/8),sqrt(pi/8))
dynamic_composition.inverse: exists on the image of each chart interval because T_beta is strictly monotone; a global R-to-R inverse additionally requires surjectivity
dynamic_composition.structure: lifted composition law of orientation-preserving time diffeomorphisms; beta=-one_half log(T_prime) is constrained by T

full_metric_pullback.off_diagonal_orbit: closed exactly, including time-depth and any h_phiA-induced time-transverse terms
full_metric_pullback.scope: stationary F_beta-invariant spatial seed and fixed F3 time action

seal_and_action.action: not selected; covariance of an eventual action and boundary functional remains open
seal_and_action.seal: time-dependent relational shift does not derive a map to the absolute static Phi=0 seal

time_map.constant_limit: beta constant gives T_beta=exp(-2beta)t plus an additive origin
time_map.monotonicity: dT_beta/dt>0 for every finite real beta
time_map.rejected_shortcut: T=exp(-2beta(t))t has an extra -2t beta_dot term

trajectory_causality.proper_time: d tau/dt=exp(-beta) sqrt(1-w^2) on the timelike branch
trajectory_causality.scope: observer-trajectory tangent only; not a material speed law or derived light dynamics
